summary: Updated durable docs to reflect the homepage hero image implementation and current live workshop details, and refreshed operational status to remove bootstrap-only placeholder text.
work_completed: Reviewed `.control-tower/packets/inbox/builder-add-homepage-banner-image-result.json` plus the current `assets/main.css` and `index.html` state before editing docs.
Updated `docs/product-specs/workshop-website.md` to document the repository-managed homepage banner image, current hero logistics copy, and the now-live organizer contact details.
Updated `docs/design-docs/site-architecture.md` to record the banner asset, CSS overlay approach, and narrow-screen banner behavior.
Updated `.control-tower/docs/state/current-status.md` to reflect the current homepage state and the remaining placeholder areas.
Confirmed no new durable doc page was needed, so docs indices did not require changes.

findings.message: Resolved doc drift in `docs/product-specs/workshop-website.md`, which still described banner logistics and contact details as placeholders despite the live homepage showing concrete values.
No docs index refresh was necessary because the changes fit existing pages and no new durable doc was added.
The product spec still treats venue, speakers, schedule details, and submission instructions as pending; those sections will need another docs pass once organizers confirm final content.

follow_up_recommendations: Run another Scribe follow-up when schedule and submission details are finalized so the durable spec and status docs can drop the remaining placeholder language.
Do a quick browser verification pass to confirm the documented mobile banner crop and text reflow match the deployed rendering.
memory_worthy: Homepage hero now depends on the repository asset `assets/atlas-banner.png` plus gradient overlays in `assets/main.css` for text legibility across desktop and mobile layouts.
Current live homepage state: static single-page site with confirmed hero date/logistics and organizer contacts, but schedule and submissions remain placeholder content.
